game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **Five Nights at Freddy's 3**: Players are tasked with surviving five consecutive nights, each lasting from 12 AM to 6 AM, within a horror attraction office, primarily fending off a single animatronic, Springtrap. Gameplay involves tapping on a tablet UI to switch between camera feeds to track Springtrap's movement and access two critical systems: an audio system to lure him away from the office, and a vent system to seal off his access points. Throughout the night, various "phantom" animatronics will appear, causing system errors (audio, camera, or ventilation) that must be rebooted by tapping a maintenance panel, consuming valuable time. Failure to manage these systems or successfully lure Springtrap away will result in him entering the office and delivering a jumpscare, leading to immediate game over. Progression is achieved by surviving each night, with difficulty scaling through increased animatronic aggression, faster system degradation, and more frequent phantom appearances.
game_info.description: Survive five nights in a haunted horror attraction office from 12 AM to 6 AM each night. Track the deadly animatronic Springtrap through security cameras and use audio lures to keep him away from your office. Seal vents to block his entry routes. Phantom animatronics will cause system failures that must be rebooted quickly. If Springtrap reaches your office, it's game over. Each night gets progressively harder with faster system failures and more aggressive behavior.
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Navigate between camera feeds and systems on tablet
Space: Activate selected system (play audio lure, seal vent, reboot system)
Z: Toggle tablet view on/off
Enter: Start game from main menu
ESC: Pause/Unpause game
R: Restart and return to main menu

TEST_1.test_description: Basic movement and interaction test. Validates that the player can navigate the tablet interface, switch between cameras, and interact with basic systems. Tests fundamental game mechanics work correctly.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Navigate through all camera feeds systematically. Toggle tablet on and off. Attempt to use audio lures and vent seals. Verify system interactions respond correctly to inputs.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Player successfully navigates all interface elements. Systems respond to inputs appropriately. No crashes or errors occur during basic interactions. Test completes without errors.

TEST_1.expected: Player successfully navigates all interface elements. Systems respond to inputs appropriately. No crashes or errors occur during basic interactions. Test completes without errors.

TEST_2.test_description: Win condition validation test. Tests optimal survival strategy to complete Night 1 successfully. Validates that proper management of Springtrap's position, audio lures, and system reboots leads to victory.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Monitor Springtrap's position constantly via cameras. Use audio lures strategically when he approaches the office. Seal vents preemptively when he's nearby. Quickly reboot systems when phantoms cause failures. Maintain awareness of time progression.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Successfully survive from 12 AM to 6 AM on Night 1. Springtrap never reaches the office. All system failures are handled promptly. Game transitions to GAME_OVER_WIN state with victory message.

TEST_3.test_description: System management stress test. Tests ability to handle multiple simultaneous system failures and rapid response requirements. Validates reboot mechanics work under pressure.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Focus on rapid system recovery when phantoms appear. Practice quick navigation to maintenance panel and efficient rebooting. Balance between monitoring Springtrap and fixing systems.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Successfully reboot all systems when failures occur. Maintain functional systems while tracking Springtrap. Demonstrate ability to multitask effectively without losing to system neglect.
